name: Goals Records and a Wild Matchday Four
angle: A serious recap of the opening matchdays at World Cup 2026, built on a wild Matchday Four: Germany's 7-1 demolition of debutants Curacao, Sweden's biggest World Cup haul since 1938, Ivory Coast's last-gasp winner, Japan's late draw with the Netherlands and Scotland's first World Cup win in 36 years, with the reigning champions and France still to begin on Tuesday.

questions.question: Which side recovered from conceding the opening goal to thrash World Cup debutants Curacao 7-1?
Who were the reigning world champions going into the 2026 World Cup?
Scotland celebrated their first World Cup win in 36 years. Which side did they edge 1-0?
Japan snatched a 2-2 draw with the Netherlands thanks to an 88th-minute equaliser. Who scored it?
Brazil were held to a 1-1 draw by Morocco. Which forward scored their equaliser?
Ivory Coast beat Ecuador 1-0 with a dramatic 90th-minute winner. Who scored it?
Sweden's 5-1 rout of Tunisia was their biggest haul in a single World Cup match since which tournament?
The United States opened with a 4-1 win over Paraguay. Which striker scored their two first-half goals?
Qatar claimed the first World Cup point in their history with a 1-1 draw against which European side?
France begin their campaign on Tuesday against an African side they famously lost to in the 2002 opener. Who?
In Germany's 7-1 win over Curacao, one forward scored twice, including a penalty. Who was it?
The USA sealed their 4-1 win over Paraguay with a stoppage-time strike curled in with the outside of the boot. Who scored it?
South Korea came from behind to beat Czechia 2-1. Which Czech captain had given his side an early lead?
The reigning champions open their title defence on Tuesday in Kansas City against which African nation?
Curacao stunned Germany by taking a shock early lead before collapsing. Which player scored that goal?

sources: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_FIFA_World_Cup_Group_E (Germany 7-1 Curacao: Comenencia 21' for Curacao, Havertz 45+5' pen. and 88'; Ivory Coast 1-0 Ecuador, Diallo 90')
